name: Uncle Bo's
tagline: Kaimuki pupu bar with creative local-Asian fusion — the garlic truffle fries and dynamite are legendary.
mustOrder: Garlic truffle fries — crispy, truffled, and addictive
Dynamite — baked seafood with spicy mayo, a local izakaya classic
Bo's fried rice — loaded with whatever's good, different every time
parking: Street parking on Waialae Ave and side streets. Kaimuki is tight — circle once or twice.
talkStory: Uncle Bo's is the Kaimuki spot for late-night grinds and drinks with friends. Chef Bo Pathammavong brings Lao and local flavors together in creative pupu that are perfect for sharing. The vibe is loud, fun, and always packed on weekends. Great for groups — order a bunch of small plates and share everything. They're open late by Honolulu standards which makes it a go-to after other restaurants close. Reservations recommended on weekends.
